completeness.rationale: The triage captured the CSV import trigger, special character content, approximate task count (~200), timing correlation, and encoding-related error flash. However, it missed two expected items: (1) the manual-save vs. auto-save distinction was never established — the summary says 'crashes on save' but doesn't clarify this is toolbar Save only, not auto-save; (2) platform (macOS 14.2) and app version (v2.3.1) were never obtained and are explicitly listed as information gaps.

accuracy.rationale: The root cause hypothesis — serializer failing on non-standard Unicode characters from the CSV import — correctly maps to the first acceptable diagnostic path (CSV import as trigger, encoding as mechanism). The agent did not discover the 64KB payload size threshold, but that is explicitly permitted by the acceptable paths. The hypothesis is directionally correct and would lead a developer to the right code area.

thoroughness.rationale: The agent asked four substantive rounds of questions and reached a reasonable hypothesis. However, it never asked about the manual-save vs. auto-save distinction (a meaningful diagnostic question), never obtained app version or OS, and never tested whether a small number of tasks with the same special characters also crashes (which would have helped isolate encoding from size). These are non-trivial gaps that the information_gaps section acknowledges but should have been pursued.

economy.rationale: All four question turns were well-targeted and each moved the diagnostic forward. The first question was somewhat generic, but that is appropriate for a cold start on a vague report. No questions were redundant or repeated. The test proposal in turn 4 was both an information-gathering move and a workaround suggestion — efficient use of a turn.

question_quality.rationale: The third question — probing the CSV origin, field types, and presence of special characters — was the standout. The reporter explicitly said 'I don't see why that would matter,' meaning the agent surfaced information the reporter would not have volunteered. The fourth question (small-list test) was also well-framed as a diagnostic experiment. Questions were professional and empathetic throughout, though the first question was somewhat generic.

actionability.rationale: The summary provides clear reproduction steps, a specific root cause hypothesis pointing to the serialization path, a recommended fix with three concrete candidate locations, and a proposed test case including a round-trip check. A developer could open the serializer and start reading immediately. The missing platform/version and auto-save distinction would have made this a 5, but the core direction is solid.

notable_strengths: Correctly identified the CSV import as the timing trigger and non-standard Unicode characters as the likely encoding mechanism, matching an acceptable diagnostic path without leading questions
Proposed a concrete small-list test that simultaneously confirmed the data-dependent hypothesis and gave the reporter a partial workaround
The final summary is detailed and structured — reproduction steps, fix candidates, test case, and information gaps are all present
Maintained empathy and reporter rapport while extracting technical specifics from a frustrated, non-technical user
notable_weaknesses: Never asked for app version or OS/platform — these were left as information gaps despite being straightforward to ask
Never established whether the crash is specific to manual Save (toolbar) vs. auto-save, which is one of the expected extraction items
Did not probe whether a small number of tasks with the same special characters also crashes, which would have helped isolate the encoding vs. size dimension of the bug
most_insightful_question: Could you tell me a bit more about that import? For instance, where did the CSV come from (another app, a spreadsheet, hand-edited?), and did it include anything beyond simple task names — like descriptions with special characters, dates, links, or any unusually long fields?
missed_opportunities: Ask whether auto-save (if it exists) triggers the same crash — would distinguish manual-save-specific behavior
Ask for app version and operating system — basic diagnostic info never collected
Ask whether creating just 5–10 tasks manually with curly quotes and em dashes also causes a crash — would have helped isolate encoding from the size/payload threshold
